At this step, it is important to know if your systems are configured with ODATA IPS connector or SCIM IPS connector to synchronize users between SAP SuccessFactors, IAS, and SAP Analytics Cloud tenant.  If you are Using ODATA IPS connector, you will need to ensure below permissions. • Manage Integration Tool- Allow Admin to Access OData API through Basic Authentication • Manage User Employee Export Using SCIM IPS connector? SCIM IPS connector with client certificate authentication, does not have any pre-requisites of permissions.

For a more detailed description of step 3 along with system screenshots, I invite you to read through page 12 of the IDP.

Step 4: Grant Story report permissions

We strongly recommend following the help guide documentation to set up permission related to Story Reports. Some modules (ex. RCM/PM/GM etc.) require additional permissions to ensure a user can report on schema specific data. Link to help guide - Set up Permission for Story report.

There are other topics of interest like the FAQs, important notes on if your systems are configured on SCIM IPS connector and you want to ensure permission-based sync relevant configurations and attribute, performing user synchronization that are mentioned in the IDP.

entry.title: Setting up Open Content Network Implementation
entry.content:
Are you looking to implement Open Content Network (OCN) as a content vendor who can make their learning courses available on SAP SuccessFactors Learning? The SuccessFactors Learning OCN option helps to track learners’ online learning without having to create or import the content. Signing up with an Open Content Network (OCN) provider and implementing the connection between SAP SuccessFactors Learning and the provider makes this possible. The provider hosts the content and manages the content. SAP SuccessFactors Learning tracks the learners' content completion. The learning administrators select the content that they want to track and then assign that content like any other learning item in SAP SuccessFactors Learning to their employees.

Here I am introducing the high-level steps needed for OCN implementation and for a full-fledged discussion I invite you to read the Implementation Design Principle (IDP) which is authored by my former colleague Anand Athanur (SAP) with contributions from Janine Zeh (ORBIS), Paul Nolan (SAP) and Karen Perez (SAP).

1. Creating an OCN Administrator

To manage the courses, you need an OCN admin user. We recommend creating one admin user per vendor (content provider). Make sure to create a new OCN role and assign to the OCN admin user.

  •  The first step is to create a role with permissions for the OCN Admin user.
  • The second step is to create an OCN admin user and associate the role from step 1.
2. Registering the OCN Provider in Learning Administration

Please read the official document on Implementing Open Content Network to register the OCN provider in LMS including labels and logos.

3. Enabling an Open Content Network Third-Party Provider

Navigate to System Administration > Configuration > System Configuration > OPEN CONTENT NETWORK .

  • Supply the values for the new OCN vendor as shown below and remember to click on “Apply Changes” to store the values.
  • If the vendor you want to enable is pre-existing, for example, LYNDA,  or others, you may simply need to enable them.

Setting up values for the OCN Vendor

Once complete, navigate to Content > Open Content Network and verify if the new OCN provider appears on the screen as shown below.

 

Confirmation screen after registering the OCN vendor

  1. Switching to BizX Token Server

SAP SuccessFactors announced the deprecation of the Learning Token Server endpoint and hence all legacy API calls using the Learning Token Server is obsolete. We do not recommend using the Learning Token Server at all. Instead, all calls must refer to using the BizX Token Server. If you as a partner have been using the Learning Token Server, you must switch to the BizX Token Server. For detailed guidance led by system screenshots, please read through section 5.2.4 of the IDP.

5. Invoking OCN OData API calls

To make OCN OData API calls, you must follow the four steps shown below:

  • Generate SAML Assertion
  • Obtain SAML Token
  • (Optional) Validate SAML Token
  • Invoke OCN OData API calls

For detailed guidance led by system screenshots on invoking ODATA APIs, please read through section 5.2.5 of the IDP.

6. Including your AIR Key in all API calls

As SAP continues it’s digital transformation, it has introduced a new requirement for all partner applications that leverage the OData APIs to include what’s known as the Application Interface Register key, abbreviated as AIR Key. Please read the announcement here. 

7. Invoking a sample OData API call using the token from step 5

Copy the access token generated before and paste it to Authorization: Bearer + + access_token If your setup is correct, the response should resemble the screenshot. The response is displayed when you request the OCN metadata.

 

Sample API call to obtain OCN metadata via a token

8. Importing OCN metadata into SAP SuccessFactors Learning

The figure below shows a sample screenshot of the OCN metadata payload for importing OCN content into SAP SuccessFactors Learning.

 

Screenshot showing partial OCN metadata payload

Following considerations are important.

  • The authorization is Bearer + “ “+ Token if you are using raw or the same as in figure 5-27 if you are using simply setting Authorization to Bearer Token. Simply copy the token from the initial call. 
  • The [ and ] brackets in the request payload indicates that you can import an array of content separated by commas, up to a maximum of 1000 per import.
  • You must wait for another 5 minutes to do another batch of imports. This is strictly enforced by the system. No exceptions!
  • The full payload is documented in the OCN Implementation Guide. 
  • Start with a small batch to ensure that your payload structure is correct. 
  • The two URLs included in launchURL are visible externally. 
  • You must adjust your content to comply with additional locales as needed.
9. Verifying imported OCN metadata in SAP SuccessFactors Learning Administration

After importing OCN metadata into SAP SuccessFactors Learning, you can verify the contents by navigating to Content > Open Content Network as shown below and selecting the OCN vendor you have created.

 

Verifying the OCN metadata and optionally importing into LMS

For modifying Content Object and changing completion option for learning content universally or individually, please follow section 5.2.9 in the IDP that includes system screenshots making for an easier understanding.

10. Re-importing or Synchronizing metadata

Situations may arise where you may wish to update previously imported metadata in the Learning Admin UI. To do so, log in as an administrator and navigate to the System Administration interface. Navigate to the Automatic Processes tab and select “Open Content Network Content Synchronization”. Mark the checkbox for “Schedule This Process”:

  • Schedule as Daily
  • Confirm the time zone is correct
  • Modify the time to a few minutes in the future, then click “Apply Changes” to save.
 

Synchronizing metadata

11. Adding OCN Items to a Library

One way to manage bulk assignments is to associate the OCN courses with a Library. Navigate to Learning Administration > Learning Activities > Libraries and click on “Add New” .

 

Adding a new Library

12. Assigning the OCN Library to an Assignment Profile

You can assign an entire Library to an user/assignment Profile or assign individual items to a user/assignment profile.

If you have followed all the steps described above, you have successfully set up an OCN vendor in your customer’s tenant. The system is now ready for learners to undergo your OCN courses at your website. Congratulations!

SAP SuccessFactors Learning will not permit you to proxy in as another user to complete learning assignments. It’s therefore necessary to log in as the learner into the platform to undergo the learning assignment. Please refer to https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2440835 for more information.

For a detailed description with system screenshots and further topics, I invite you to explore the IDP. Let us know in the comments how has been your experience in implementing an OCN.

entry.title: Configuring country specific Rehire Verification Process
entry.content:
Introduction:

Rehiring is the process where organizations hire their former employees again back to their organization. When it happens, the former employees as an external candidates get hired across legal entities where they worked as an employee before. Since the candidates can be from different Legal entities from different countries, it is good to assign their Rehiring process to the country specific Onboarding Manager groups as similar as country specific Onboarding process to maintain the process as simple as Onboarding process for Onboarding Managers.

Onboarding supports this process by assigning the Rehire verification process to the country specific Onboarding Managers to carry forward with the Rehiring process.

Pre-requisites:

  1. Configuring Rehire check.
  2. Enabling permission to Rehire Inactive Employee (by ‘match’ in New Hire) and Rehire Inactive Employee with New Employment (by ‘match’ in New Hire) (as per the requirement).
  3. Creating Rehire Coordinator responsible group or repurposing the Country specific Onboarding Manager groups (as per the requirement).
  4. Configure Rehire Coordinator Business rule.
  5. Enabling Rehire with Old employment switch in Onboarding General settings.

Note: Onboarding HR Managers who are going to review the Rehire verification process, must have required permission for external candidate’s Personal Information and Job information. Mostly, Onboarding Managers who review New Hire data must be having the permission already.

For more information about the pre-requisite and overview about Rehire with Old and New employment: Rehire with SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding: From Exit to Re-entry 

 

Configuration for Country specific Rehire verification process:

  • Create a country specific Rehire verification group for Onboarding Managers.

Note: You can reuse the country specific Onboarding Managers group that are already created for country specific Onboarding process for this Rehire assignment rule or else we can create a country specific group for Rehire verification process specifically.

Responsible group

  •  Creating a business rule

To assign the Rehire verification process, create a business rule that validates the country of a legal entity for which the candidate is getting hired for and then assign that country specific Onboarding Managers group like given in the screen shot.

 Business rule configuration

  •  Configure the business rule in Onboarding configuration:

Once the rule is created, assign this rule to get trigger.

Navigate to Manage Data → Search Onboarding Configuration → Select the Default Object → Search for - Rule for Rehire Coordinator Group Configuration and assign the rule in the field → Save.

Recruit/Rehire Configuration

Expected result:

After the configuration setup, once the onboarding is initiated for the candidate, system validates whether the candidate is a Rehire or not. If the candidate is a Rehire, system assigns the Rehire verification process to that country specific Onboarding HR Managers for the Profile Verification Process.

Example:

Onboarding Manager added to ONB HR Manager USA group.

 Rehire Verification Process is assigned to the same Onboarding HR Manager who is part of USA group.

 

 

 

I hope this document is clear and informative, I would like to hear your thoughts and feedback on this topic. So, feel free to share your thoughts in the comments. 

Thanks for reading this document!

entry.title: Configure the Employee Central Benefit Type - Reimbursements; for Beginners Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:

I would like to share my wonderful experience towards the “Global Employee Benefits” to the Beginners of Employee Central Consultant who has passionate on the Benefits Topic.

Most of them will not get the opportunity to know about the Benefits Topic/Product Features and they will not be in the comfortable zone to discuss the features in the Implementation Project > Specifically in the Fit to Standard Workshop.

The idea here is to enable the Consultant to know the basic overview of the “Benefit” topic and start discussing the same with Customer (in the Project to make use of this module and provide the extended user experience).

It is highly recommended to suggest the Customer to use the SAP SuccessFactors Best Practices and Approaches to strengthen the HR Process in the Organization.

The following are the Benefit Types in SuccessFactors.

  1. Allowance
  2. Reimbursement
  3. Pension/Savings/Retirement Plan
  4. Insurance
  5. Wallet
  6. Deductible Allowance
  7. Savings Plan
  8. Others

Most of the countries extensively can use the “Reimbursement/Claim and Insurance Types since it has the features to automate End-to-End Business Process for the customers.

As a beginner, you must talk about the benefits module and how this can be helpful for the HR Business Process, how they can strengthen HR Process and automate the claim process with end-to-end process without manual intervention.

Let us start with the easy one (Reimbursement Benefit Type), How to Configure the Business Process, How to Configure the Simple Business Rule and Enable the Role Based Permissions.

Business Requirements:

Our customer is having the following requirements / cases.

  • Air Ticket Reimbursement (this is used as a Sample Cases to share the look and feel of the configurations and understand the configurations steps)
  • 2 Level of Approval Process (Workflow)
  • Restriction or Validations by Using the Business Rule
  • Custom UI Screen High level Screen Shot
  • User Experience

Here, we are going to see the step-by-step configuration process and how to enable this functionality to Business User and Employees (Including the Workflow and Business Rules)

Section 1: Workflow and Business Rule Configuration

 

Note: You can add the validations based on the business needs. Here I have restricted with claim ID.

Note: There are two level of approvals been configured, this needs to be deployed into the benefits object (Under Validate and Save Rules respectively) as per the below screen shots.

Section 2: Step-by-Step Configuration Process

Goto > Benefit Admin Overview

Step 1 & Step 2 Specify the Benefit Type and General Information’s

Select the Benefit Type from the Dropdown Value (Reimbursement)

Enter the General Information’s as above Benefit ID, Name, and Effective from Date and Choose the Relevant Currency to display in the claim)

In Eligibility Criteria, always the entitlement amount should be -1.

In Payroll Integration Choose from the Dropdown Values (Integration with Payroll or Benefits)

Step 3: Associate the Legal Entities

Here, you have to choose and associate the listed Legal Entity which are eligible for this reimbursement.

If there more legal entities, but only few of them are eligible to claim this process, in this case you have to choose the relevant legal entities from the list. So that it will be showed only those entities employees.

Step 4: Specify Enrollment and Claim Dates

Create the Benefit Schedule, Select the Frequency from the Dropdown Values and Days Remaining to Claim into Show.

Step 5 & 6: Define Enrollment Details & Add Useful Contacts

Choose, whether enrolment required or now, here we let us use no required.

Choose the Contact Person from the List (or create it by adding.

Step 7: Hyperlinks and Policy Documents

The below steps will be very useful the end user to view the Policy Documents, Links, URL etc. towards the reimbursements (you can add the policy documents based on the different reimbursement)

Step: 8 & 9 Specify Claim / Reimbursement Details (if Benefit Type is Reimbursement)

Here, you need to choose the Claim Screen ID (Standard), if you have configured any custom UI, please select the UI to display into Users.

Note: There are some customers, might ask for more fields in the claim screen and wants to validate or add etc. so you have to ensure the new fields in the object level and create the UI accordingly and specify the respective claim screen ID as highlighted above.

In Benefit Payment Options, Choose the Pay Component which needs to be aligned for the Reimbursement, Choose the Payment Mode (Payroll), Ensure the Default into Yes.

Step 10: Other Options

There are below exception options towards the benefit for exceeded amount and approval exceptions, let us not look it now and go without selections.

Configurations Steps are completed now, and you must enable the Role Based Permission to provide access to the Employees, and Approvers respectively.

Section 3: One of the Sample UI (Customized Solution) which mentioned in the step 8.

 

Section 4: User Experience: In Employee / Administrator View, User will create the claim as per the below screen shots.

Approver’s View

I hope, the above information will be useful for the community and start their journey towards the Benefits Module (Business Requirements, Workflow, Business Rules and Validations, and how to deploy the same into Configure Object Definition along with User Experience.

Kindly request you all to share your and valuable feedback and suggestions to strengthen the blog and motivate me to do such more blogs.

Thank You.

Anba ( Anbazhagan N)

entry.title: EC Time Off Rounding of Accruals - Detailed Example
entry.content:
Introduction

Business rules in time off can be tricky based on the customers' requirements, and rounding of accruals may be one of them. There are couple of notes and blogs regarding this topic. (The most generic one can be found here.) In this article, I wanted to share a bit more complicated example with detailed explanations. You will see that the business rule is straight-forward easy; and compared to that, the scenario requires more attention.

Scenario
In our example, employee earns total of 20 days of accrual within a year. We want to have a hire and/or a termination rule, and want to recalculate accruals based on couple of conditions.

If the employee worked for a full year, the accrual will be 20 days, and will be prorated based on applicable months. As an example, for 10 months of work, the prorated amount will be: 20 / 12 x 10 = 16,67.

Note: The calculation of the total number of months within a year where employee was applicable for accrual is not the subject of this article. This value will be stored in a standard variable, which will be used within a business rule.

By looking at the example above, we can then create a table as follows:

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,673,3356,678,331011,6713,331516,6718,3320

Now comes the tricky part: The customer wants to change its approach and adjust these amounts, so that:

  1. Decimals below 0,25 should be rounded to 0.
  2. Decimals between 0,25 to 0,75 should be rounded to 0,5.
  3. Decimals above 0,75 should be rounded to 1.

So the desired accrual amounts will be as follows:

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,53,556,58,51011,513,51516,518,520

The following section will be about how to achieve this.

Implementation

The business rule that we are going to use can be found below.

Now let us breakdown the rule in details.

  1. The number of months is our variable as mentioned before, and this stores the total number of applicable months. This value is then multiplied by the results of 20 / 12.
  2. Then we multiply this result by 2. The 2 here is selected because it will help us with rounding the decimals later on, as there are 2 x 0,5 within 1, and we want decimals to be either 0, or 0,5.
  3. Afterwards we round the decimals, and lastly we divide the value by 2 again, as we already multiplied them.

The result of these operations can be found below:

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,673,3356,678,331011,6713,331516,6718,3320
x 2 3,336,661013,3316,662023,3326,663033,3336,6640
Round3710131720232730333740
/ 21,53,556,58,51011,513,51516,518,520

As seen, we now have the desired results, and job's done!

If you have different rounding criterions, you can easily adjust the number 2 we used above, and change this value according to your requirements. As an example, if you want to round the accruals to the closest 0,2 fractions, then you can use number 5 while multiplying and dividing.

Months123456789101112
Accrual1,673,3356,678,331011,6713,331516,6718,3320
x 58,3316,662533,3341,665058,3366,667583,3391,66100
Round817253342505867758392100
/ 51,63,456,68,41011,613,41516,618,420

 

Summary

I always have fun during adjusting the business rules and brainstorm about such calculations. I hope that this article will be helpful for you during your projects! Please also check the other blogs related to this topic.

Also please feel free to give feedback or comment.

Best,
Altug
